_meta.description: Beyond Paradise Marine Wildlife Encounter Index — species × location × month × sighting-probability × legality × ethics. This is the keystone niche asset.
_meta.rules: Ethics graded on observable criteria only. Every encounter must link to ≥1 peer-reviewed source or official regulation. Re-verify sighting probabilities every season.

locations.probability_notes: ~180–200 individually catalogued sharks in Tanzania waters (Sharkbook citizen science). Peak aggregations around Mafia December–January coincide with spawning events.
locations.permit_authority: MPRU Tanzania (Marine Parks and Reserves Unit)

restrictions: No touching whale sharks
Minimum 3m distance from body, 4m from tail
Maximum 6 swimmers per individual at once
No flash photography underwater
No motorised underwater vehicles within 50m
Vessels must cut engine when within 50m of animal
red_flags: Groups of 10+ swimmers crowding a single animal
ethics.grade_notes: Permitted under Tanzania Marine Parks guidelines. Verify individual operator compliance — not all enforce distance rules equally.

sources.name: Marine Megafauna Foundation — Tanzania whale shark research
Wildbook for Whale Sharks (sharkbook.ai) — Tanzania sightings database

species.tim_firsthand_notes: Seen from Matlai beach frequently. Menai Bay pod observable most mornings from October to March with a good guide.

locations.probability_notes: A resident pod of 200–400 individuals (Tursiops aduncus + Stenella longirostris mixed group) inhabits Menai Bay year-round. Tourism-impact studies document boat disturbance is a concern — ethical operator selection critical.

restrictions: Vessels must not pursue dolphins
Engines off when dolphins approach
No swimming if dolphins show avoidance behaviour (direction change, speed increase, submerging)
MBCA recommends max 1 boat at any dolphin group
Morning tours only (dolphins more active and boats fewer)
red_flags: Multiple boats surrounding a single group
Boats revving engines to keep pace with dolphins
Operators permitting swimming when dolphins are clearly avoiding
ethics.grade_notes: PLOS ONE (Amir et al. 2016) documents significant behavioural disruption in Menai Bay. The experience is available but operator selection is the most important factor — choose MBCA-registered operators only.

key_study.citation: Amir OA et al. (2016) Tourism impact on dolphin behaviour in Menai Bay, Zanzibar

sources.name: PLOS ONE — Amir et al. 2016, Tourism impact on Indo-Pacific bottlenose dolphins in Menai Bay
Menai Bay Conservation Area — official MBCA management guidelines

species.tim_firsthand_notes: Seen in mixed groups with bottlenose dolphins in Menai Bay. Spinner dolphins rest/rest-travel during daytime — morning encounters are calmer.

locations.probability_notes: Mixed groups with T. aduncus. Spinner dolphins are more wary of boats than bottlenose — respect their approach distance.

restrictions: Same as T. aduncus — MBCA guidelines apply
Particularly avoid approaching resting groups

species.tim_firsthand_notes: Regular at Mnemba Atoll — feeding on seagrass beds at dawn. Nest protection active at Ras Nungwi and southern Zanzibar beaches.

locations.probability_notes: Resident population. Most encounters in early morning snorkelling on seagrass feeding grounds. Nesting October–February on surrounding beaches.

restrictions: No touching turtles
Do not block their path to the surface to breathe
Minimum 2m distance
No flash photography
On nesting beaches: no lights at night, no approaching nests
ethics.grade_notes: Encounters are passive — turtles approach snorkellers on their own terms. The ethical concern is nesting beach disturbance, not in-water interaction.

sources.name: WWF Tanzania Marine Programme — sea turtle conservation Zanzibar

locations.probability_notes: Active nest protection programme since 2017. First documented hatchlings May 2017. 2023: 4 nests recorded, 100+ hatchlings. Five IUCN-listed species in surrounding waters.
locations.permit_authority: Private island — Thanda Island access required

restrictions: Guided only on Thanda Island property
No nest disturbance
ethics.grade_notes: Conservation programme is documented and active. Best turtle encounter programme in Tanzania for accountability.

locations.probability_notes: Southern Ocean humpbacks migrate north past Tanzania coast July–September. Boat-based sightings possible from Kilwa. Not a predictable encounter — whale-watching not commercially established here yet.

restrictions: Vessels must maintain 100m distance under WCPFC guidelines
No swimming with humpbacks in Tanzanian waters — no operator currently offers this legally
ethics.grade_notes: Humpback whale swimming is permitted in some regions (Tonga, Silver Bank) under strict permits. Not established or regulated in Tanzania — observe from boat only.

species.tim_firsthand_notes: Regular sightings in Serengeti Western Corridor and Masai Mara. Pride behaviour best in dry season when grass is short.

locations.probability_notes: Estimated 3,000+ lions in the Serengeti ecosystem. Year-round, probability highest dry season (June–October) when prey concentrates near water and short grass improves visibility.

restrictions: No off-road driving in national park zones
No feeding
Maintain 20m minimum vehicle distance
ethics.grade_notes: Standard safari ethics apply. Private conservancy lodges (Singita Grumeti, Lamai) allow off-road driving — significant advantage for getting close.

sources.name: TANAPA — Serengeti National Park wildlife status

locations.probability_notes: One of the highest lion densities in Africa. Olare Motorogi and Mara North conservancies (private) allow off-road driving.

restrictions: Off-road driving permitted only in private conservancies, not public reserve

species.tim_firsthand_notes: I have tracked the migration pattern across four years. The Grumeti river crossing (June–August, Western Corridor) is less visited than Mara crossings but equally dramatic. Mara crossings (July–September) have larger herds but more vehicles.

locations.probability_notes: 1.5 million wildebeest + 200k zebra move through Western Corridor May–August. Grumeti River crossings June–July. Fewer tourist vehicles than Mara — best balance of drama and exclusivity.
locations.permit_authority: TANAPA or Singita Grumeti private concession

sources.name: Frankfurt Zoological Society — Serengeti migration ecology

locations.probability_notes: Herd arrives Kenya from July. River crossings peak August–September. Unpredictable timing (hours-to-days wait for crossing). Private conservancy gives off-road positioning advantage.

Calving season January–March: 400,000+ calves born in a 3-week window. Highest predator density in Africa during this period (lions, cheetah, hyena, wild dog). The most underrated migration experience.
locations.permit_authority: TANAPA (Ndutu is partly in NCA, partly Serengeti)

sources.name: Frankfurt Zoological Society — Serengeti migration ecology
